title: Loop controller entry
description: One bounded work loop under .praxis/loops/log.jsonl, governed by the loop-control rule. A loop iterates toward a terminal predicate (all acceptance criteria met) under a budget and a no-progress guard, and either converges (done) or escalates to the user — it never spins forever.

updated.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the last mutation.

status.description: Lifecycle: running -> done | escalated | abandoned. An escalated loop returns to running only via resume. Closed set, never invented.

goal.description: What the loop is trying to achieve.

criteria.description: The terminal predicate: every criterion must be met to finish. Must be non-empty — an empty predicate can never legitimately complete.

max_iterations.description: Budget guard: escalate once this many iterations have run without meeting the predicate.

patience.description: No-progress guard: escalate after this many consecutive iterations with an unchanged progress signature.

iterations.description: One record per tick, in order.

ts.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp of the tick.

met.description: Criterion ids met at this tick (a fresh snapshot, not cumulative).

signal.description: Free-form state fingerprint, e.g. '2 tests failing'.

verdict.description: The computed verdict for this tick. Closed set.
The latest verdict (empty before the first tick).

escalation.description: Why the loop escalated (budget exhausted or no progress), or a manual close note.
